About Me

Honest about where
I am. Serious about
where I'm going.

I'm a first-year BCA student at Amity University. I come from a commerce background — no mathematics, no prior coding experience. Everything I've built, I've figured out on my own, mostly by just starting and seeing what happens.

Last year I built VANI, a humanoid robot head — my first real hardware project. It wasn't perfect, but it worked, and more importantly, it taught me how to think across hardware and software at the same time.

Right now I'm exploring how technology can be applied in healthcare — a space I think is under-built and genuinely important. I'm also doing data analytics programs on the side because I think data and robotics are going to overlap in interesting ways.

I don't claim to know everything. I'm early. But I learn fast when I care about something, and I care about this a lot.

What I'm Currently Working On
Healthcare technology startup — early stage, building quietly

BCA — Year 1 of 3 at Amity University, Noida

Projects

Things I've actually built.

2026 Completed
VANI — Humanoid Robot Head
A functional humanoid robot head with real-time voice interaction and expressive response. Built to explore how hardware and software can work together — and what that combination could mean for human-robot interaction in healthcare and education.
Future scope: AI language model integration, emotion recognition, deployment in high-risk environments.
Arduino Servo Motors OLED Display Voice Input HTML/CSS/Java
🏛 Presented at AICraft 3.1 · Amity Centre for Artificial Intelligence

2020–
2026
Model United Nations — 7 Conferences
Participated in 7 MUN conferences across six years, representing country positions on global policy, science, and humanitarian issues. Built strong skills in independent research, structured debate, and high-pressure communication — and got comfortable defending ideas in front of a room.
